page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m2.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.e server family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.5 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance characteristics. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1199.89 and a multi-core score of 3141.11, both placing in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Passmark CPU and memory benchmarks scored 1602.78 and 573.5 respectively, placing in the bottom 10%. Data processing capabilities are modest, with single-threaded gzip compression at 21.49 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 8.45 GB/sec. Database and web serving benchmarks also reflect entry-level performance, with Redis SET operations at 1.19 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 0.24 GB/sec.

Given the shared CPU allocation and entry-level benchmark performance, this instance represents a budget-friendly option for non-critical workloads. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a compute-leaning resource density at a low entry cost, though the shared CPU model introduces potential resource contention. This server is best suited for lightweight, non-production workloads such as basic development environments, low-traffic web serving, code repositories, or simple background task processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m2.xlarge is an entry-level, x86_64 virtual machine from the ecs.e family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 4 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM, and contains no local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3141.11 and a Passmark CPU score of 1602.78. Redis SET operations reach 1.19 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput is limited to 0.24 GB/sec. Due to its shared CPU architecture and baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps, this instance is a cost-effective option designed for low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for development environments, lightweight background tasks, and low-traffic web hosting.
